blocks.content: The project wrapped on a Friday. The group went to an off-campus party to celebrate — one of those sprawling LA house parties where the backyard had string lights and someone was always playing guitar badly near the fire pit.
Cassidy found Kit near the drinks around eleven.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: You look like you're doing math in your head.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Just thinking.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: About what.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Home.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: Girlfriend back home?
He turned the cup in his hand.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Complicated.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: Complicated like you don't know what it is, or complicated like you don't want to say.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Both, maybe.
She leaned against the wall beside him. Not subtle about being beside him.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: My roommate's gone for the weekend.
He knew what that meant. She wasn't being unclear about it.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: Nobody has to know what happens here, Kit. She's all the way in Minnesota. What's a little fun gonna hurt. We don't have to tell.
He looked at her. Cassidy was watching him with an expression that was patient and certain at the same time — like she'd already decided how this ended and was waiting for him to catch up.
The worst part wasn't that she said it. The worst part was the two seconds before he answered — when he was aware, very specifically, that a choice was being made. That he was making it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I can't.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: Can't or won't.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Won't.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: She's lucky.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It's not about that.

blocks.speaker: Cassidy
blocks.content: Yeah it is. It's completely about that.

She pushed off the wall and disappeared back into the party. Kit stood where he was for a moment, then walked out to the front steps and sat down and pulled out his phone and stared at Daisy's name for a long time.
He didn't text her. Not yet. He needed to figure out what he was going to say — and what he was going to leave out.
